description_long: This is a stone Celtic cross sculpture featuring intricate relief carvings on both its front and back surfaces. The front side displays a depiction of a crucified figure, likely Jesus Christ, with a circular halo and outstretched arms, set within the cross's central circle. The lower portion of the front shows a smaller carved face or symbol. The reverse side features a relief of a standing figure holding a child, possibly representing a saint or Madonna and Child, with a carved angelic face at the top. The cross is thick and robust, with a ring connecting the arms and shaft, characteristic of traditional Celtic crosses. The surface appears weathered and textured, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete, and the overall color is a pale gray.
description: A pale gray stone Celtic cross sculpture with detailed relief carvings of religious figures on both sides and a circular ring connecting the arms. The surface is textured and weathered, giving it an aged appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the back of the cross, featuring a relief of a standing figure holding a child and an angelic face at the top.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the thickness of the cross and the depth of the relief carvings, with the circular ring structure clearly visible.
description_view_2: The front view displays the crucified figure with outstretched arms and a halo, as well as a small carved face or symbol at the base.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, showing the cross's thickness and the ring structure, with the relief details partially visible.
attribute_subject_matter: Celtic cross with religious reliefs of Jesus Christ and a Madonna and Child
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the background is isolated and does not provide environmental context.
